project.name: Flixsy
project.description: Movie discovery platform with real-time data from RapidAPI IMDB.

nodes.name: core/templates/core/detail.html
nodes.summary: Django template for the movie detail page, displaying full metadata including title, description, rating, directors, writers, genres, and release date with a navigation rail, bottom nav, topbar, and play/action buttons.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Django template variables (movie.*) and static file references for detail.css and detail.js.

nodes.name: core/templates/core/home.html
nodes.summary: Django template for the home page featuring a hero carousel, genre filter chips, movie and series card rows, with an inline JavaScript application that fetches data from RapidAPI IMDB and renders the UI dynamically.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains a large inline IIFE (648 lines) implementing the entire frontend application: API client, carousel, card rendering, genre filtering, and navigation. Hardcodes an API key for rapidapi.com.

nodes.name: core/templates/core/template.html
nodes.summary: Standalone static HTML/CSS mockup of a movie detail page, featuring a full design system with custom properties, backdrop hero, detail grid layout, cast grid, similar movies carousel, and sidebar information blocks.
nodes.languageNotes: Fully self-contained with embedded CSS (526 lines). No Django template tags or external dependencies. Uses hardcoded sample data for 'Nebula Rising'.

nodes.name: fetchFromIMDb
nodes.summary: Async function that fetches data from the IMDb RapidAPI endpoint by constructing a URL with query parameters and returning the parsed JSON response, with error handling for non-OK status codes.

nodes.name: createMediaCard
nodes.summary: Renders an HTML string for a media card component containing a thumbnail image or play icon, movie title, and a link to the detail page.

nodes.name: slideHTML
nodes.summary: Renders a full hero carousel slide HTML string with background image, title, year, rating, genre badges, description, and play/more-info action links pointing to the detail page.

nodes.name: buildHero
nodes.summary: Constructs the entire hero carousel UI by setting slide and dot HTML, resetting the slide index and auto-advance timer, and binding dot click handlers.

nodes.name: goTo
nodes.summary: Navigates the hero carousel to a specific slide index by applying a CSS transform translation and updating dot active states, then resetting the auto-advance timer.

nodes.name: fillRow
nodes.summary: Populates a media card container row with rendered card HTML, auto-removing the parent section if no items are available, and ensures the section is attached to the DOM if detached.

nodes.name: loadMovies
nodes.summary: Fetches the most popular movies from the IMDb API, normalizes the response data, builds the hero carousel from movies with images, and fills the movies row with all results.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Configuration file defining glob patterns for files and directories to exclude from understand-anything code analysis scans.
nodes.languageNotes: Same syntax as .gitignore with comment, negation, and directory markers.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty init file that marks the config directory as a Python package for the Django project.

nodes.name: asgi.py
nodes.summary: ASGI configuration entry point for the Django project, exposing the application callable for asynchronous server deployment.

nodes.name: settings.py
nodes.summary: Django project settings including installed apps, middleware, database configuration, and static file paths. Loads environment variables via dotenv and registers the core app.

nodes.name: urls.py
nodes.summary: Root URL configuration routing admin, core app URLs, and favicon redirect at the project level.

nodes.name: wsgi.py
nodes.summary: WSGI configuration entry point for the Django project, exposing the application callable for synchronous server deployment.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty init file that marks the core directory as a Python package for the Django application.

nodes.name: admin.py
nodes.summary: Django admin registration file that registers the Item model with the admin interface for CRUD operations.

nodes.name: apps.py
nodes.summary: Django application configuration for the core app, defining the default auto field and app name.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty init file that marks the migrations directory as a Python package for Django database migrations.

nodes.name: 0001_initial.py
nodes.summary: Initial Django database migration that creates the Item model with titulo, descripcion, and fecha_creacion fields.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Item Django model with titulo, descripcion, and fecha_creacion fields, configured with descending date ordering.

nodes.name: detail.css
nodes.summary: Stylesheet for the movie detail page with design tokens, dark theme, side navigation, hero section, video player, and responsive breakpoints.

nodes.name: detail.js
nodes.summary: Client-side JavaScript for the movie detail page that fetches movie data from the API, renders backdrop, poster, metadata, sidebar, and attaches an embedded video player with server selection.

nodes.name: style.css
nodes.summary: Global application stylesheet with design tokens, dark theme, navigation rail, hero carousel, media cards, genre strip, bottom nav, and responsive layout.

nodes.name: urls.py
nodes.summary: URL routing for the core app mapping home, movie detail by primary key, movie detail by IMDB ID, and JSON API endpoint paths to their view handlers.

nodes.name: views.py
nodes.summary: Core application views including HomeView listing, movie detail by primary key or IMDB ID, and a JSON API endpoint. Fetches movie data from the RapidAPI IMDB service with caching.

nodes.name: manage.py
nodes.summary: Django management command-line utility that sets the settings module and delegates to django.core.management.execute_from_command_line.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project readme describing Flixsy as a movie discovery platform with real-time data from the RapidAPI IMDB service.

nodes.name: CoreConfig
nodes.summary: Django AppConfig subclass that configures the core application with BigAutoField as the default auto field.

nodes.name: Migration
nodes.summary: Django Migration class representing the initial database migration that creates the Item model table.

nodes.name: Item
nodes.summary: Django model representing a generic item with titulo, descripcion, and fecha_creacion fields, ordered by creation date descending.

nodes.name: HomeView
nodes.summary: Django ListView subclass that renders the home page with a list of Item objects and cached movie data from the IMDB API.

nodes.name: _raise_for_auth
nodes.summary: Raises a PermissionError with a clear message when the RapidAPI response status is 401 or 403.

nodes.name: _fetch_movies
nodes.summary: Fetches a list of movies from the RapidAPI IMDB endpoint, processes raw data with pk assignment, and returns a list of movie dictionaries.

nodes.name: _fetch_movie_by_id
nodes.summary: Fetches a single movie from the RapidAPI IMDB endpoint by IMDB ID, trying multiple ID formats and returning None on failure.

nodes.name: _get_cached_movies
nodes.summary: Retrieves cached movie list from Django cache, falling back to fetching from the API and storing the result with a 15-minute timeout.

nodes.name: movie_detail
nodes.summary: View that renders the movie detail page by looking up a movie from cached data using its numeric primary key.

nodes.name: movie_detail_imdb
nodes.summary: View that renders the movie detail page by IMDB ID, checking cache first then falling back to a real-time API fetch, with series detection for season/episode context.

nodes.name: movie_api_json
nodes.summary: JSON API endpoint that returns movie detail data by IMDB ID, consumed by the client-side detail.js for dynamic rendering.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point function that configures the Django settings module and executes management commands from the command line.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: HTML templates and static assets (CSS, JS) that render the frontend for movie discovery pages including detail, home, and a static mockup.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Request handlers and URL routing that serve movie data pages and a JSON API endpoint, fetching from RapidAPI IMDB with caching.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Database models, schema migrations, and admin interface for the Item model storing movie metadata.

layers.name: Config Layer
layers.description: Project-wide Django configuration including settings, ASGI/WSGI server adapters, root URL routing, and app configuration.

layers.name: Root Layer
layers.description: Package initialization markers, CLI entry point (manage.py), and tooling configuration for code analysis.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Project-level documentation describing Flixsy as a movie discovery platform with real-time RapidAPI IMDB data.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start here to understand what Flixsy is: a movie discovery platform that pulls real-time data from the RapidAPI IMDB service. This document gives you the project's purpose, the architecture at a glance, and how to get it running — a roadmap for everything that follows.

tour.title: CLI Entry Point
tour.description: Every Django project starts with manage.py — a thin CLI wrapper that sets the DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE environment variable and delegates to django.core.management. The main() function is what runs when you type 'python manage.py runserver' or any other management command.
tour.languageLesson: Django's manage.py reads settings from the module path set in DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE. It uses os.environ.setdefault() so the environment variable takes precedence if already set — a common Python pattern for allowing overrides without code changes.

tour.title: Django Configuration & Server Setup
tour.description: The config layer wires the project together. settings.py defines installed apps, middleware, database, and static file paths. The URL configuration at config/urls.py routes requests to the admin panel and core app. ASGI and WSGI entry points let Django run under both async and sync servers. The CoreConfig in apps.py registers the core application.
tour.languageLesson: Django's settings module is a plain Python file (not YAML or JSON). This means settings can use Python expressions — like calling os.getenv() for secrets or constructing DATABASES from environment variables. ASGI/WSGI adapters decouple the server protocol from application code, letting the same app run under gunicorn, uvicorn, or Daphne.

tour.title: Data Model & Database Schema
tour.description: The Item model in models.py defines the application's data: titulo, descripcion, and fecha_creacion fields with descending date ordering. The initial migration at 0001_initial.py translates this model into a database table. Together they form the persistence layer that stores movie metadata from the IMDB API.
tour.languageLesson: Django ORM models define both the Python API and the database schema in one class. Each model field becomes a database column, and the Meta class handles cross-cutting configuration like ordering and indexes. Migrations track schema evolution — every field change creates a new migration file, enabling version-controlled database history.

tour.title: Admin Interface
tour.description: Django's built-in admin panel gives staff users CRUD access to the Item model without writing any frontend code. This single-file registration unlocks a full data management UI — useful for manually correcting movie entries and verifying API-fetched data.

tour.title: View Layer — Request Handlers
tour.description: core/views.py is the heart of the server-side application. It contains HomeView (a ListView subclass) that renders the home page with cached movie data, plus function-based views for movie details and a JSON API. The URL configuration at core/urls.py maps each route to its view, connecting the web requests to the handlers that serve them.
tour.languageLesson: Django's class-based ListView abstracts pagination, queryset filtering, and template resolution. Overriding get_context_data is the hook for injecting additional data — here, cached IMDB results get merged with database QuerySet results before hitting the template.

tour.title: API Data Flow — Caching & Fetching
tour.description: These four private functions form the data pipeline from RapidAPI IMDB to your browser. _raise_for_auth handles 401/403 responses. _fetch_movies and _fetch_movie_by_id make the actual HTTP requests. _get_cached_movies wraps everything with Django's cache framework, storing results for 15 minutes to stay within RapidAPI's free tier limits.
tour.languageLesson: The cache-and-fallback pattern here separates concerns: the caller always asks the cache first, which delegates to the fetcher only on cache miss. This keeps API call frequency predictable and the view code simple — no cache-checking logic leaks into the view handlers.

tour.title: Detail Views & JSON Endpoint
tour.description: Three views handle movie detail display. movie_detail looks up a movie by its numeric primary key from cached data. movie_detail_imdb fetches by IMDB ID with series detection for season/episode content. movie_api_json returns raw JSON for the client-side JavaScript to consume — creating a clean API boundary between server-rendered templates and dynamic frontend components.

tour.title: Home Page Frontend Application
tour.description: The home page template contains a complete inline JavaScript application inside a Django template. loadMovies orchestrates the entire page: it calls fetchFromIMDb to get data, passes results to buildHero for the hero carousel, and fills media card rows via fillRow. This self-contained architecture avoids a separate SPA framework while delivering a dynamic, API-driven experience.
tour.languageLesson: Inline IIFE patterns keep all JavaScript scoped to the template, avoiding global namespace pollution. Async/await with try/catch wraps the fetch API calls, and DOM manipulation happens directly via innerHTML and classList — no virtual DOM, no framework overhead. This pattern works well for Django templates that need just-enough interactivity.

tour.title: Movie Detail Page — UI & Client Logic
tour.description: When a user clicks a movie card, detail.html renders the full metadata view. The page loads detail.css for dark-themed styling and detail.js for client-side rendering — fetching movie data from the JSON API endpoint (Step 8), building the backdrop, poster, sidebar, and attaching an embedded video player with server selection.
tour.languageLesson: Django templates use {{ variable }} and {% tag %} syntax. The detail template extends a base layout, iterates over genre lists with {% for %}, and conditionally renders sections with {% if %}. Static files are referenced via {% static %} — which resolves to the path configured in settings.STATIC_URL.

tour.title: Global Styles & Design Mockup
tour.description: The global stylesheet establishes the design system: dark theme, navigation rail, hero carousel, media cards, genre strip, and bottom nav — all responsive across device sizes. The standalone template.html is a pure HTML/CSS design mockup that prototypes the detail page layout and visual design independently of the Django backend, serving as a design reference for the production templates.
tour.languageLesson: CSS custom properties (design tokens) keep the theme consistent. Variables like --color-primary and --spacing-unit are defined once in :root and referenced throughout. The dark theme uses a [data-theme='dark'] attribute selector — switching themes requires changing one attribute on the document element rather than toggling individual class names.
